                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills around 0 degrees expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of east central Georgia and central South\nCarolina.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frostbite and hypothermia will occur if unprotected skin\nis exposed to these temperatures.                 "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 5:14AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Raleigh NC",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills as low as 5 below.\n\n* WHERE...All of central North Carolina.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frostbite and hypothermia will occur if unprotected skin\nis exposed to these temperatures. An extended period of freezing\ntemperatures could cause ruptured water pipes.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors.                 "senderName": "NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 5:08AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 3 above. For the first Freeze Warning, sub-freezing\ntemperatures as low as 18. For the second Freeze Warning,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 21 expected. For the Cold\nWeather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 13 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of northeast Florida and southeast Georgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning.\nFor the first Freeze Warning, until 11 AM EST this morning. For\nthe second Freeze Warning and Cold Weather Advisory, from 7 PM\nthis evening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...The dangerously cold wind chills as low as 5 above zero\ncould cause frostbite on exposed skin in as little as 30 minutes.\nVery cold temperatures can lead to hypothermia with prolonged\nexposure. Appropriate action should be taken to ensure tender\nvegetation and outdoor pets have adequate protection from the cold\ntemperatures. Young children, the elderly and the homeless are\nespecially vulnerable to the cold. Take measures to protect them.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {

                        "NPWJAX"
                    ],

                    ],

                "event": "Freeze Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "headline": "Freeze Warning issued February 1 at 5:08AM EST until February 1 at 11:00AM EST by NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 3 above. For the first Freeze Warning, sub-freezing\ntemperatures as low as 18. For the second Freeze Warning,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 21 expected. For the Cold\nWeather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 13 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of northeast Florida and southeast Georgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning.\nFor the first Freeze Warning, until 11 AM EST this morning. For\nthe second Freeze Warning and Cold Weather Advisory, from 7 PM\nthis evening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...The dangerously cold wind chills as low as 5 above zero\ncould cause frostbite on exposed skin in as little as 30 minutes.\nVery cold temperatures can lead to hypothermia with prolonged\nexposure. Appropriate action should be taken to ensure tender\nvegetation and outdoor pets have adequate protection from the cold\ntemperatures. Young children, the elderly and the homeless are\nespecially vulnerable to the cold. Take measures to protect them.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.",
                "response": "Execute",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWJAX"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS72 KJAX 011008"
                    ],
                    "NWSheadline": [

                    ],

                        "EAS",
                        "NWEM",

                "event": "Cold Weather Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued February 1 at 5:08AM EST until February 2 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 1 above. For the first Freeze Warning, sub-freezing\ntemperatures as low as 17. For the second Freeze Warning,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 19 expected. For the Cold\nWeather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 12 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of northeast and northern Florida and southeast\nGeorgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning.\nFor the first Freeze Warning, until 11 AM EST this morning. For\nthe second Freeze Warning and Cold Weather Advisory, from 7 PM\nthis evening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...The dangerously cold wind chills as low as zero could\ncause frostbite on exposed skin in as little as 30 minutes. Very\ncold temperatures can lead to hypothermia with prolonged exposure.\nAppropriate action should be taken to ensure tender vegetation and\noutdoor pets have adequate protection from the cold temperatures.\nYoung children, the elderly and the homeless are especially\nvulnerable to the cold. Take measures to protect them.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWJAX"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS72 KJAX 011008"
                    ],

                "category": "Met",
                "severity": "Severe",
                "certainty": "Likely",
                "urgency": "Expected",
                "event": "Extreme Cold Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 5:08AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 1 above. For the first Freeze Warning, sub-freezing\ntemperatures as low as 17. For the second Freeze Warning,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 19 expected. For the Cold\nWeather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 12 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of northeast and northern Florida and southeast\nGeorgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning.\nFor the first Freeze Warning, until 11 AM EST this morning. For\nthe second Freeze Warning and Cold Weather Advisory, from 7 PM\nthis evening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...The dangerously cold wind chills as low as zero could\ncause frostbite on exposed skin in as little as 30 minutes. Very\ncold temperatures can lead to hypothermia with prolonged exposure.\nAppropriate action should be taken to ensure tender vegetation and\noutdoor pets have adequate protection from the cold temperatures.\nYoung children, the elderly and the homeless are especially\nvulnerable to the cold. Take measures to protect them.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.",
                "response": "Prepare",

                "event": "Freeze Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "headline": "Freeze Warning issued February 1 at 5:08AM EST until February 2 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Jacksonville FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 1 above. For the first Freeze Warning, sub-freezing\ntemperatures as low as 17. For the second Freeze Warning,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 19 expected. For the Cold\nWeather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 12 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of northeast and northern Florida and southeast\nGeorgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning.\nFor the first Freeze Warning, until 11 AM EST this morning. For\nthe second Freeze Warning and Cold Weather Advisory, from 7 PM\nthis evening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...The dangerously cold wind chills as low as zero could\ncause frostbite on exposed skin in as little as 30 minutes. Very\ncold temperatures can lead to hypothermia with prolonged exposure.\nAppropriate action should be taken to ensure tender vegetation and\noutdoor pets have adequate protection from the cold temperatures.\nYoung children, the elderly and the homeless are especially\nvulnerable to the cold. Take measures to protect them.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.",
                "response": "Execute",
                "parameters": {

                    ],

                "headline": "Coastal Flood Advisory issued February 1 at 2:02AM PST until February 1 at 10:00AM PST by NWS Seattle WA",
                "description": "* WHAT...Minor coastal flooding expected. Inundation of around 1\nto 2 feet above ground level is possible along shorelines and\nlow-lying coastal areas.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of northwest and west central Washington near\nthe Salish Sea, Strait of Juan de Fuca, Puget Sound and Hood\nCanal.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM PST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Minor coastal flooding due to tidal overflow is\nexpected around high tide. This may lead to flooding of\nparking lots, parks, and roads, with only isolated road\nclosures expected.",
                "instruction": "If travel is required, allow extra time as some roads may be\nclosed. Do not drive around barricades or through water of\nunknown depth.                 "headline": "High Surf Advisory issued February 1 at 1:47AM PST until February 3 at 9:00AM PST by NWS Los Angeles/Oxnard CA",
                "description": "* WHAT...Large breaking waves of 8 to 11 feet along the Central\nCoast and 5 to 8 feet along the Ventura Coast with dangerous rip\ncurrents. Surf will be highest along west and northwest facing\nbeaches.\n\n* WHERE...San Luis Obispo County Beaches, Santa Barbara County\nCentral Coast Beaches and Ventura County Beaches.\n\n* WHEN...From 6 AM this morning to 9 AM PST Tuesday.\n\n* IMPACTS...There is an increased risk for ocean drowning. Rip\ncurrents can pull swimmers and surfers out to sea. Large\nbreaking waves can cause injury, wash people off beaches and\nrocks, and capsize small boats near shore. Minor coastal\nflooding of beaches.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Additionally, minor coastal flooding will\nbe possible around the morning high tides on Sunday and Monday,\nhighest Monday. High tides will be around 6.5 to 7.0 feet\nbetween 830 and 930 am both days.",
                "instruction": "Remain out of the water due to dangerous surf conditions, or stay\nnear occupied lifeguard towers. Rock jetties can be deadly in\nsuch conditions, stay off the rocks.",

                "event": "Beach Hazards Statement",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Los Angeles/Oxnard CA",
                "headline": "Beach Hazards Statement issued February 1 at 1:47AM PST until February 2 at 9:00PM PST by NWS Los Angeles/Oxnard CA",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerous rip currents and breaking waves due to\nelevated surf expected.\n\n* WHERE...Malibu Coast and Los Angeles County Beaches.\n\n* WHEN...From late tonight through Monday evening.\n\n* IMPACTS...There is an increased risk of ocean drowning. Rip\ncurrents can pull swimmers and surfers out to sea. Waves can\nwash people off beaches and rocks, and capsize small boats\nnearshore. Minor coastal flooding of beaches.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Additionally, very minor coastal flooding\nwill be possible around the morning high tides on Sunday and\nMonday, highest Monday. High tides will be around 6.8 to 7.1\nfeet between 815 and 900 am both days.",
                "instruction": "Remain out of the water due to hazardous swimming conditions, or\nstay near occupied lifeguard towers. Rock jetties can be deadly\nin such conditions, stay off the rocks.",
                "response": "Avoid",
                "parameters": {

                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 4:44AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Baltimore MD/Washington DC",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills as low as 22 below.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of western Maryland, central, northwest, and\nwestern Virginia, and eastern West Virginia.\n\n* WHEN...Until 1 PM EST this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...The cold weather could result in hypothermia or\nfrostbite on exposed skin in as little as 30 minutes.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.",

                "headline": "Beach Hazards Statement issued February 1 at 1:16AM PST until February 2 at 9:00PM PST by NWS San Diego CA",
                "description": "* WHAT...Breaking waves of 4 to 6 feet with sets to 7 feet on\nwest-facing beaches will combine with higher than average tides\nand lead to minor tidal overflow and local beach flooding.\n\n* WHERE...Orange County Coastal Areas.\n\n* WHEN...From late tonight through Monday evening.\n\n* IMPACTS...Minor tidal overflow, beach flooding, and coastal\nerosion most likely during times of high tides.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Predicted high tides at Newport Beach: 6.4\nfeet (MLLW) at 08:58 AM PST Monday.",
                "instruction": "Remain out of the water to avoid hazardous swimming conditions.",
                "response": "Avoid",

                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 4:12AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Charleston SC",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nin the single digits above zero this morning. For the Cold Weather\nAdvisory, very cold wind chills in the lower to mid teens tonight.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of southeast Georgia and southeast South Carolina.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST this afternoon.\nFor the Cold Weather Advisory, from 6 PM this evening to 9 AM EST\nMonday.\n\n* IMPACTS...An extended period of freezing temperatures could cause\nruptured water pipes.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must go\noutside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.",

                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued February 1 at 4:12AM EST until February 2 at 9:00AM EST by NWS Charleston SC",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nin the single digits above zero this morning. For the Lake Wind\nAdvisory, northwest winds 15 to 25 knots with gusts up to 30 knots\nand waves 2 to 3 feet. For the Cold Weather Advisory tonight, very\ncold wind chills in the lower to mid teens.\n\n* WHERE...Inland Berkeley County.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST this afternoon.\nFor the Lake Wind Advisory, until 10 AM EST this morning. For the\nCold Weather Advisory, from 6 PM this evening to 9 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Strong winds and rough waves on Lake Moultrie will create\nhazardous conditions for small craft. Frostbite and hypothermia\ncould occur if unprotected skin is exposed to these temperatures.\nAn extended period of freezing temperatures could cause ruptured\nwater pipes.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...The combination of gusty winds and\nsub-freezing temperatures may result in areas of freezing spray\nover the lake and along the lakeshore.",
                "instruction": "Boaters on Lake Moultrie should use extra caution since strong winds\nand rough waves can overturn small craft.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must go\noutside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nin the single digits above zero this morning.                 "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must go\noutside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.",

                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 4:12AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Charleston SC",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nin the single digits above zero this morning. For the Lake Wind\nAdvisory, northwest winds 15 to 25 knots with gusts up to 30 knots\nand waves 2 to 3 feet. For the Cold Weather Advisory tonight, very\ncold wind chills in the lower to mid teens.\n\n* WHERE...Inland Berkeley County.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST this afternoon.\nFor the Lake Wind Advisory, until 10 AM EST this morning. For the\nCold Weather Advisory, from 6 PM this evening to 9 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Strong winds and rough waves on Lake Moultrie will create\nhazardous conditions for small craft. Frostbite and hypothermia\ncould occur if unprotected skin is exposed to these temperatures.\nAn extended period of freezing temperatures could cause ruptured\nwater pipes.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...The combination of gusty winds and\nsub-freezing temperatures may result in areas of freezing spray\nover the lake and along the lakeshore.",
                "instruction": "Boaters on Lake Moultrie should use extra caution since strong winds\nand rough waves can overturn small craft.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must go\noutside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.",

                "description": "* WHAT...Minor saltwater flooding expected.\n\n* WHERE...Florida Keys.\n\n* WHEN...Through this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Minor saltwater flooding of low elevation streets and\nlots is likely.                 "headline": "Wind Advisory issued February 1 at 4:03AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Key West FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Cold Weather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low\nas 26. For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds 15 to 25 mph with\ngusts up to 30 mph.\n\n* WHERE...Monroe Upper Keys.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result. Very\ncold temperatures can lead to hypothermia with prolonged exposure.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.",
                "response": "Execute",

                "senderName": "NWS Charleston WV",
                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued February 1 at 3:55AM EST until February 1 at 11:00AM EST by NWS Charleston WV",
                "description": "* WHAT...Very cold wind chills as low as 10 below expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of northeast Kentucky, southeast Ohio, southwest\nVirginia, and central, northeast, northern, southeast, southern,\nand western West Virginia.\n\n* WHEN...Until 11 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...The cold wind chills as low as 10 below zero could\nresult in hypothermia if precautions are not taken.",
                "instruction": "Keep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nAdditional information can be found at https://www.weather.gov/rlx\nas well as on our X and Facebook pages.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Gale Warning, northwest winds 15 to 25 kt with\ngusts up to 40 kt and waves 2 to 5 ft. For\nthe Freezing Spray Advisory, until 10 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in freezing spray is hazardous.\nFreezing spray may render mechanical and electronic components\ninoperative. Ice accretion on decks and superstructures may\nresult in some loss of stability. Strong winds will cause\nhazardous waves which could capsize or damage vessels.",

                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Baltimore MD/Washington DC",
                "headline": "Heavy Freezing Spray Advisory issued February 1 at 3:51AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Baltimore MD/Washington DC",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Gale Warning, northwest winds 15 to 25 kt with\ngusts up to 40 kt and waves 2 to 4 ft. For the Freezing Spray\nAdvisory, moderate accumulation of freezing spray. For the Low\nWater Advisory, abnormally low water levels expected.\n\n* WHERE...Chesapeake Bay from Pooles Island MD to North Beach\nMD, Chester River to Queenstown MD, and Eastern Bay.\n\n* WHEN...For the Gale Warning, until midnight EST tonight. For\nthe Freezing Spray Advisory, until 10 AM EST this morning. For\nthe Low Water Advisory, until 4 PM EST this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in freezing spray is hazardous.\nFreezing spray may render mechanical and electronic components\ninoperative. Ice accretion on decks and superstructures may\nresult in some loss of stability. Strong winds will cause\nhazardous waves which could capsize or damage vessels. Below\nnormal water levels will result in hazardous navigating\nconditions.",
                "instruction": "Mariners should prepare for ice accumulation on their vessel and\nconsider altering plans to avoid or mitigate these hazardous\nconditions.\n\nMariners should alter plans to avoid these hazardous conditions.\nRemain in port, seek safe harbor, alter course, and/or secure the\nvessel for hazardous conditions.\n\nMariners should navigate shallow waters with extreme caution due\nto below normal water levels.",

                "event": "Cold Weather Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Jackson MS",
                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued February 1 at 2:00AM CST until February 1 at 12:00PM CST by NWS Jackson MS",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills as low as 3 degrees are\nexpected.  Lows will be in the teens this morning.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of southeast Arkansas, northeast Louisiana, and\ncentral, east central, north central, northeast, northwest, south\ncentral, southeast, southwest, and west central Mississippi.\n\n* WHEN...Until noon CST today.\n\n* IMPACTS...An extended period of freezing temperatures could cause\nruptured water pipes. Very cold wind chills and temperatures can\nlead to frostbite and hypothermia, especially with prolonged\nexposure and if unprotected skin is exposed to these temperatures\nand wind chills. Extensive power outages and snow and ice cover\npersist from previous ice storm. This dangerous cold will only\nexacerbate the impacts felt in those affected areas.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nDrain in-ground sprinkler systems and cover above ground pipes.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nMonitor the latest forecasts and warnings for updates.",
                "response": "Prepare",

                "event": "Rip Current Statement",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS San Juan PR",
                "headline": "Rip Current Statement issued February 1 at 3:52AM AST until February 1 at 6:00PM AST by NWS San Juan PR",
                "description": "* WHAT...Life-threatening rip currents.\n\n* WHERE...Northwest to northeast beaches of Puerto Rico,\nincluding Culebra & the northern U.S. Virgin Islands.\n\n* WHEN...Until 6 PM AST this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Rip currents can sweep even the best swimmers away\nfrom shore into deeper water, where it becomes difficult to\nreturn to safety.",
                "instruction": "There is a high risk of rip currents.\n\nRip currents are powerful channels of water flowing quickly away\nfrom shore, which occur most often at low spots or breaks in the\nsandbar and in the vicinity of structures such as groins, jetties\nand piers. Heed the advice of lifeguards, beach patrol flags and\nsigns.\n\nIf you become caught in a rip current, yell for help. Remain\ncalm, do not exhaust yourself and stay afloat while waiting for\nhelp. If you have to swim out of a rip current, swim parallel to\nshore and back toward the beach when possible. Do not attempt to\nswim directly against a rip current as you will tire quickly.",
                "response": "Avoid",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "CFWSJU"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WHCA42 TJSJ 010752"
                    ],
                    "NWSheadline": [
                        "HIGH RIP CURRENT RISK NOW IN EFFECT THROUGH THIS AFTERNOON"
                    ],
                    "BLOCKCHANNEL": [
                        "EAS",
                        "NWEM",
                        "CMAS"
                    ],
                    "VTEC": [
                        "/O.EXT.TJSJ.RP.S.0004.000000T0000Z-260201T2200Z/"
                    ],
                    "eventEndingTime": [

                    ],

                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC",
                "headline": "Winter Storm Warning issued February 1 at 2:25AM EST until February 1 at 7:00AM EST by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC",
                "description": "* WHAT...Light additional snow expected, mainly along the Interstate\n77 corridor. Blowing snow and areas of black ice expected.\n\n* WHERE...Northeast Georgia, the foothills and Piedmont of North\nCarolina, and Upstate South Carolina.\n\n* WHEN...Until 7 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Following heavy snowfall, many roads are treacherous.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Very gusty winds are producing areas of\nblowing snow, which will cause very poor visibility at times\novernight.",
                "instruction": "Monitor the latest forecasts for updates on this situation.\n\nSlow down and use caution while traveling.\n\nIf you must travel, keep an extra flashlight, food, and water in\nyour vehicle in case of an emergency.",
                "response": "Prepare",

                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Melbourne FL",
                "headline": "Rip Current Statement issued February 1 at 2:20AM EST until February 2 at 3:00AM EST by NWS Melbourne FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerous rip currents, rough surf and a southward-\nflowing longshore current.\n\n* WHERE...Beaches of Volusia, Brevard, Indian River, St Lucie, and\nMartin Counties.\n\n* WHEN...Through late tonight.\n\n* IMPACTS...Rip currents can sweep even the best swimmers away\nfrom shore into deeper water.",
                "instruction": "Rip currents are powerful channels of water flowing quickly away\nfrom shore, which occur most often at low spots or breaks in the\nsandbar and in the vicinity of structures such as jetties and\npiers. Heed the advice of lifeguards, beach patrol flags and\nsigns.\n\nEntering the surf is strongly discouraged. If caught in a rip\ncurrent, relax and float. Don't swim against the current. If\nable, swim in a direction following the shoreline. If unable to\nescape, face the shore and call or wave for help.",
                "response": "Avoid",
                "parameters": {

                        "CFWMLB"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [

                "event": "Extreme Cold Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Melbourne FL",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 2:17AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Melbourne FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds 20 to 25 mph with\ngusts up to 35 mph. For the first Extreme Cold Warning through\nthis morning, dangerously cold wind chills as low as 9 to 15\ndegrees. For the first Freeze Warning through this morning, hard\nfreezing temperatures as low as 22 to 27 degrees. For the second\nExtreme Cold Warning tonight, dangerously cold wind chills as low\nas 14 to 20 degrees expected. For the second Freeze Warning\ntonight, hard freezing temperatures as low as 22 to 27 degrees\nexpected.\n\n* WHERE...All counties in east central Florida, including Volusia,\nLake, Seminole, Orange, Brevard, Osceola, Indian River,\nOkeechobee, Saint Lucie, and Martin.\n\n* WHEN...For the Wind Advisory, until 7 AM EST this morning. For the\nfirst Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning. For the\nfirst Freeze Warning, until 11 AM EST this morning. For the second\nExtreme Cold Warning and second Freeze Warning, from 7 PM this\nevening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nDangerously cold wind chills could lead to frostbite and\nhypothermia, even with limited exposure. Hard freeze conditions\ncould kill crops, other sensitive vegetation, and possibly damage\nunprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nIf you do not have heat in your home or lack shelter, you can call\n211 or visit your local county website for assistance locating\nappropriate shelter from the cold.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold. Bring pets\nand potted plants indoors. Check on elderly neighbors to make sure\ntheir furnace is working. To prevent freezing and possible bursting\nof outdoor water pipes, they can be wrapped, drained or allowed to\ndrip slowly.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWMLB"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS72 KMLB 010717"
                    ],
                    "NWSheadline": [
                        "WIND ADVISORY REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 7 AM EST THIS MORNING... ...EXTREME COLD WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 10 AM EST THIS MORNING... ...FREEZE WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 11 AM EST THIS MORNING... ...EXTREME COLD WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT FROM 7 PM THIS EVENING TO 10 AM EST MONDAY... ...FREEZE WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT FROM 7 PM THIS EVENING TO 10 AM EST MONDAY"

                "urgency": "Expected",
                "event": "Freeze Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Melbourne FL",
                "headline": "Freeze Warning issued February 1 at 2:17AM EST until February 1 at 11:00AM EST by NWS Melbourne FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds 20 to 25 mph with\ngusts up to 35 mph. For the first Extreme Cold Warning through\nthis morning, dangerously cold wind chills as low as 9 to 15\ndegrees. For the first Freeze Warning through this morning, hard\nfreezing temperatures as low as 22 to 27 degrees. For the second\nExtreme Cold Warning tonight, dangerously cold wind chills as low\nas 14 to 20 degrees expected. For the second Freeze Warning\ntonight, hard freezing temperatures as low as 22 to 27 degrees\nexpected.\n\n* WHERE...All counties in east central Florida, including Volusia,\nLake, Seminole, Orange, Brevard, Osceola, Indian River,\nOkeechobee, Saint Lucie, and Martin.\n\n* WHEN...For the Wind Advisory, until 7 AM EST this morning. For the\nfirst Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning. For the\nfirst Freeze Warning, until 11 AM EST this morning. For the second\nExtreme Cold Warning and second Freeze Warning, from 7 PM this\nevening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nDangerously cold wind chills could lead to frostbite and\nhypothermia, even with limited exposure. Hard freeze conditions\ncould kill crops, other sensitive vegetation, and possibly damage\nunprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nIf you do not have heat in your home or lack shelter, you can call\n211 or visit your local county website for assistance locating\nappropriate shelter from the cold.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold. Bring pets\nand potted plants indoors. Check on elderly neighbors to make sure\ntheir furnace is working. To prevent freezing and possible bursting\nof outdoor water pipes, they can be wrapped, drained or allowed to\ndrip slowly.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Execute",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWMLB"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS72 KMLB 010717"
                    ],
                    "NWSheadline": [

                "event": "Wind Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Melbourne FL",
                "headline": "Wind Advisory issued February 1 at 2:17AM EST until February 1 at 7:00AM EST by NWS Melbourne FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds 20 to 25 mph with\ngusts up to 35 mph. For the first Extreme Cold Warning through\nthis morning, dangerously cold wind chills as low as 9 to 15\ndegrees. For the first Freeze Warning through this morning, hard\nfreezing temperatures as low as 22 to 27 degrees. For the second\nExtreme Cold Warning tonight, dangerously cold wind chills as low\nas 14 to 20 degrees expected. For the second Freeze Warning\ntonight, hard freezing temperatures as low as 22 to 27 degrees\nexpected.\n\n* WHERE...All counties in east central Florida, including Volusia,\nLake, Seminole, Orange, Brevard, Osceola, Indian River,\nOkeechobee, Saint Lucie, and Martin.\n\n* WHEN...For the Wind Advisory, until 7 AM EST this morning. For the\nfirst Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning. For the\nfirst Freeze Warning, until 11 AM EST this morning. For the second\nExtreme Cold Warning and second Freeze Warning, from 7 PM this\nevening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nDangerously cold wind chills could lead to frostbite and\nhypothermia, even with limited exposure. Hard freeze conditions\ncould kill crops, other sensitive vegetation, and possibly damage\nunprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nIf you do not have heat in your home or lack shelter, you can call\n211 or visit your local county website for assistance locating\nappropriate shelter from the cold.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold. Bring pets\nand potted plants indoors. Check on elderly neighbors to make sure\ntheir furnace is working. To prevent freezing and possible bursting\nof outdoor water pipes, they can be wrapped, drained or allowed to\ndrip slowly.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Execute",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWMLB"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS72 KMLB 010717"
                    ],
                    "NWSheadline": [
                        "WIND ADVISORY REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 7 AM EST THIS MORNING... ...EXTREME COLD WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 10 AM EST THIS MORNING... ...FREEZE WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT UNTIL 11 AM EST THIS MORNING... ...EXTREME COLD WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT FROM 7 PM THIS EVENING TO 10 AM EST MONDAY... ...FREEZE WARNING REMAINS IN EFFECT FROM 7 PM THIS EVENING TO 10 AM EST MONDAY"

                "senderName": "NWS Tiyan GU",
                "headline": "Small Craft Advisory issued February 1 at 5:10PM ChST until February 3 at 6:00AM ChST by NWS Tiyan GU",
                "description": "* WHAT...Northeast winds 15 to 25 kt with occasional gusts up to\n35 kt and seas 8 to 11 feet.\n\n* WHERE...Marianas Coastal Waters.\n\n* WHEN...Until 6 AM ChST Tuesday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...A shear line remains across the region and\nwill continue to produce gusty showers and hazardous marine\nconditions of 15 to 25 kt winds and seas of 8 to 11 feet\nthrough Monday night. Current guidance suggest these conditions\nwill improve Tuesday as the shear line weakens and the northerly\nswell diminishes.",
                "instruction": "Inexperienced mariners, especially those operating smaller\nvessels, should avoid boating in hazardous conditions.",

                "senderName": "NWS Wakefield VA",
                "headline": "High Surf Advisory issued February 1 at 2:09AM EST until February 2 at 4:00AM EST by NWS Wakefield VA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the High Surf Advisory, large breaking waves of 8\nto 12 feet expected in the surf zone. For the Coastal Flood\nWarning, two to three feet of inundation above ground level\nexpected in low-lying areas near shorelines and tidal\nwaterways.\n\n* WHERE...Eastern Currituck County.\n\n* WHEN...For the Coastal Flood Warning, until 11 AM EST this\nmorning. For the High Surf Advisory, until 4 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Widespread flooding of vulnerable areas will result\nin an elevated threat of property damage to homes and\nbusinesses near the waterfront and shoreline. Water will be 2\nto 3 feet above ground level in some areas resulting in a\nsufficient depth to close numerous roads and threaten homes\nand businesses. Flooding will extend inland from the\nwaterfront along tidal rivers and bays resulting in some road\nclosures and flooding of vehicles. Dangerous swimming and\nsurfing conditions and localized beach erosion.",
                "instruction": "Take the necessary actions to protect flood-prone property. If\ntravel is required, do not drive around barricades or through\nwater of unknown depth.\n\nInexperienced swimmers should remain out of the water due to\ndangerous surf conditions.",
                "response": "Avoid",

                "senderName": "NWS Wakefield VA",
                "headline": "Coastal Flood Advisory issued February 1 at 2:09AM EST until February 1 at 11:00AM EST by NWS Wakefield VA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the High Surf Advisory, large breaking waves of 7\nto 11 feet expected in the surf zone. For the Coastal Flood\nAdvisory, up to one foot of inundation above ground level\nexpected in low-lying areas near shorelines and tidal\nwaterways.\n\n* WHERE...In Maryland, the Maryland Beaches. In Virginia,\nAccomack County.\n\n* WHEN...For the High Surf Advisory, until 4 AM EST Monday. For\nthe Coastal Flood Advisory, until 11 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Shallow flooding is expected in the most vulnerable\nlocations near the waterfront and shoreline resulting in a low\nthreat of property damage. Expect up to one foot of water\nabove ground level in low lying, vulnerable areas. Some roads\nand low lying property including parking lots, parks and lawns\nnear the waterfront will experience shallow flooding.\nDangerous swimming and surfing conditions and localized beach\nerosion.",
                "instruction": "If travel is required, allow extra time as some roads may be\nclosed. Do not drive around barricades or through water of\nunknown depth. Take the necessary actions to protect flood-prone\nproperty.\n\nInexperienced swimmers should remain out of the water due to\ndangerous surf conditions.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the High Surf Advisory, large breaking waves of 8 to\n12 feet expected in the surf zone. For the Coastal Flood\nWarning, one to three feet of inundation above ground level\nexpected in low-lying areas near shorelines and tidal waterways.\n\n* WHERE...Virginia Beach.\n\n* WHEN...For the High Surf Advisory, until 4 AM EST Monday. For\nthe Coastal Flood Warning, until 1 PM EST this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Widespread flooding of vulnerable areas will result\nin an elevated threat of property damage to homes and\nbusinesses near the waterfront and shoreline. Water will be 1\nto 3 feet above ground level in some areas resulting in a\nsufficient depth to close numerous roads and threaten homes\nand businesses. Flooding will extend inland from the\nwaterfront along tidal rivers and bays resulting in some road\nclosures and flooding of vehicles. Dangerous swimming and\nsurfing conditions and localized beach erosion.",
                "instruction": "Take the necessary actions to protect flood-prone property. If\ntravel is required, do not drive around barricades or through\nwater of unknown depth.\n\nInexperienced swimmers should remain out of the water due to\ndangerous surf conditions.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the High Surf Advisory, large breaking waves of 8\nto 12 feet expected in the surf zone.                 "headline": "Coastal Flood Advisory issued February 1 at 2:07AM EST until February 1 at 12:00PM EST by NWS Mount Holly NJ",
                "description": "* WHAT...Up to one foot of inundation above ground level\nexpected in low-lying areas near shorelines and tidal\nwaterways.\n\n* WHERE...Inland Sussex and Delaware Beaches.\n\n* WHEN...Until noon EST today.\n\n* IMPACTS...At this level, flooding begins on the most\nvulnerable roads in coastal and bayside communities, and along\ninland tidal waterways. Some partial or full road closures are\npossible.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Minor coastal flooding may linger near\nthe times of high tide through Monday. The highest water\nlevels and greatest impacts are forecast to occur during the\nSunday morning high tide, when isolated Moderate coastal flood\nimpacts are possible along the back bays. Ice from the back\nbays could get pushed onshore during high tide, and flood\nwaters could freeze on roadways, further complicating impacts.",
                "instruction": "A Coastal Flood Advisory means that minor tidal flooding is\nexpected. Minor tidal flooding often results in some road\nclosures. Usually, the most vulnerable roadways will flood.\n\nDo not leave your vehicle at a location that is prone to tidal\nflooding. Do not drive your vehicle through flood waters. The\nwater may be deeper than you think it is. You will be putting\nyourself in danger and your vehicle may be damaged, leading to\ncostly repairs.\n\nVisit the National Water Prediction Service at\nhttps://water.noaa.gov/wfo/phi for additional water level and\nflood impact information for your local tide gauge.",

                "event": "Coastal Flood Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Mount Holly NJ",
                "headline": "Coastal Flood Advisory issued February 1 at 2:07AM EST until February 1 at 12:00PM EST by NWS Mount Holly NJ",
                "description": "* WHAT...Up to one half foot of inundation above ground level\nexpected in low-lying areas near shorelines and tidal\nwaterways.\n\n* WHERE...Ocean and Coastal Ocean.\n\n* WHEN...Until noon EST today.\n\n* IMPACTS...At this level, flooding begins on the most\nvulnerable roads in coastal and bayside communities, and along\ninland tidal waterways. Some partial or full road closures are\npossible.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Minor coastal flooding may linger near\nthe times of high tide through Monday. The highest water\nlevels and greatest impacts are forecast to occur during the\nSunday morning high tide, when isolated Moderate coastal flood\nimpacts are possible along the back bays. Ice from the back\nbays could get pushed onshore during high tide, and flood\nwaters could freeze on roadways, further complicating impacts.",
                "instruction": "A Coastal Flood Advisory means that minor tidal flooding is\nexpected. Minor tidal flooding often results in some road\nclosures. Usually, the most vulnerable roadways will flood.\n\nDo not leave your vehicle at a location that is prone to tidal\nflooding. Do not drive your vehicle through flood waters. The\nwater may be deeper than you think it is. You will be putting\nyourself in danger and your vehicle may be damaged, leading to\ncostly repairs.\n\nVisit the National Water Prediction Service at\nhttps://water.noaa.gov/wfo/phi for additional water level and\nflood impact information for your local tide gauge.",

                "senderName": "NWS Mount Holly NJ",
                "headline": "Heavy Freezing Spray Warning issued February 1 at 2:00AM EST until February 2 at 6:00AM EST by NWS Mount Holly NJ",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Gale Warning, north winds 25 to 30 kt with\ngusts up to 40 kt and seas 7 to 10 ft. For the Heavy Freezing\nSpray Warning, heavy freezing spray at a rate of 2 cm per hour\nor greater may rapidly accumulate on vessels.\n\n* WHERE...Coastal waters from Little Egg Inlet to Great Egg\nInlet NJ out 20 nm, Coastal waters from Great Egg Inlet to\nCape May NJ out 20 nm, Coastal waters from Cape May NJ to Cape\nHenlopen DE out 20 nm and Coastal waters from Cape Henlopen to\nFenwick Island DE out 20 nm.\n\n* WHEN...For the Gale Warning, until 6 PM EST this evening. For\nthe Heavy Freezing Spray Warning, until 6 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in heavy freezing spray is\nhazardous. Freezing spray may render mechanical and electronic\ncomponents inoperative. Rapid ice accretion on decks and\nsuperstructures may result in a catastrophic loss of\nstability. Strong winds will cause hazardous seas which could\ncapsize or damage vessels and reduce visibility.",
                "instruction": "Mariners should prepare for dangerous accumulation of ice on\ntheir vessel. If possible, remain in port, avoid the warning area\nor conduct mitigation.\n\nMariners should alter plans to avoid these hazardous conditions.\nRemain in port, seek safe harbor, alter course, and/or secure the\nvessel for hazardous conditions.",

                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Mount Holly NJ",
                "headline": "Gale Warning issued February 1 at 2:00AM EST until February 1 at 6:00PM EST by NWS Mount Holly NJ",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Gale Warning, north winds 25 to 35 kt with\ngusts up to 40 kt and seas 6 to 9 ft. For the Heavy Freezing\nSpray Warning, heavy freezing spray at a rate of 2 cm per hour\nor greater may rapidly accumulate on vessels.\n\n* WHERE...Coastal waters from Sandy Hook to Manasquan Inlet NJ\nout 20 nm and Coastal waters from Manasquan Inlet to Little\nEgg Inlet NJ out 20 nm.\n\n* WHEN...For the Gale Warning, until 6 PM EST this evening. For\nthe Heavy Freezing Spray Warning, until 6 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in heavy freezing spray is\nhazardous. Freezing spray may render mechanical and electronic\ncomponents inoperative. Rapid ice accretion on decks and\nsuperstructures may result in a catastrophic loss of\nstability. Strong winds will cause hazardous seas which could\ncapsize or damage vessels and reduce visibility.",
                "instruction": "Mariners should prepare for dangerous accumulation of ice on\ntheir vessel. If possible, remain in port, avoid the warning area\nor conduct mitigation.\n\nMariners should alter plans to avoid these hazardous conditions.\nRemain in port, seek safe harbor, alter course, and/or secure the\nvessel for hazardous conditions.",
                "response": "Avoid",

                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued February 1 at 1:58AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Mount Holly NJ",
                "description": "* WHAT...Very cold wind chills as low as 7 below.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of central and northern Delaware, northeast\nMaryland, central and southern New Jersey, and southeast\nPennsylvania.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...The cold wind chills as low as 5 below zero could result\nin hypothermia if precautions are not taken. Frostbite and\nhypothermia will occur if unprotected skin is exposed to these\ntemperatures.",
                "instruction": "Use caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly.                 "description": "* WHAT...For the Cold Weather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low\nas 20 below.                 "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside.                 "headline": "Coastal Flood Advisory issued January 31 at 10:55PM PST until February 1 at 12:00PM PST by NWS Eureka CA",
                "description": "* WHAT...High astronomical tides will cause minor flooding in\nlow lying areas around Humboldt Bay. Up to half foot of\nsaltwater inundation above ground level is possible near\nshorelines and tidal waterways. Near 8.8 feet MLLW at the\nNorth Spit tide gauge is predicted.\n\n* WHERE...Northern Humboldt Coast County.\n\n* WHEN...From 9 AM to noon PST Sunday. High tide is at 10:42 AM\nat the Humboldt Bay North Spit tide gauge. Saltwater\ninundation will be possible 1 to 2 hours before and after high\ntide.\n\n* IMPACTS...Flooding of low lying areas around Humboldt Bay\nincluding parks and roads with only isolated closures\nexpected. Flooding will likely impact Jackson Ranch Road in\nthe Arcata Bottoms and several roads in King Salmon.",

                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Boston/Norton MA",
                "headline": "Coastal Flood Advisory issued February 1 at 1:52AM EST until February 2 at 2:00PM EST by NWS Boston/Norton MA",
                "description": "* WHAT...One to two feet of inundation above ground level\nexpected in low-lying areas near shorelines and tidal\nwaterways (10.8 to 12.7 feet Mean Lower Low Water).\n\n* WHERE...Eastern Essex MA, Suffolk MA, Eastern Norfolk MA,\nEastern Plymouth MA, Barnstable MA and Dukes MA Counties.\n\n* WHEN...From 8 AM this morning to 2 PM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Shallow pockets of flooding less than one foot deep\naffects the lowest lying areas along the coast, including\nMorrissey Boulevard in Boston. Most roads remain passable.\nFlooding up to one foot deep affects coastal roads on the\nNorth Shore from Salem to Gloucester and Newburyport. Rough\nsurf will cause flooding on some coastal roads around the time\nof high tide due to splashover. Roads remain passable with\npockets of shallow flooding less than one foot deep. This\nmainly affects low lying areas and roads near Edgartown\nHarbor, including the Chappy Ferry Dock. Shallow flooding may\nalso approach Five Corners in Vineyard Haven.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Sub-freezing temperatures and cold road\nsurfaces may allow for some of the storm surge to freeze.\nThis would result in prolonged issues on those vulnerable roadways\nand also delay drainage.",
                "instruction": "If travel is required, allow extra time as some roads may be\nclosed. Do not drive around barricades or through water of\nunknown depth. Take the necessary actions to protect flood-prone\nproperty.",
                "response": "Monitor",
                "parameters": {

                "description": "* WHAT...One to two feet of inundation above ground level\nexpected in low-lying areas near shorelines and tidal\nwaterways (6.0 to 6.5 feet Mean Lower Low Water).\n\n* WHERE...Nantucket MA County.\n\n* WHEN...From 8 AM this morning to 2 PM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Many roads near Nantucket Harbor become impassable\nwith 1 to 2 feet of water and affects access to the ferry\nterminals.                 "event": "Freezing Fog Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Albuquerque NM",
                "headline": "Freezing Fog Advisory issued January 31 at 11:51PM MST until February 1 at 8:00AM MST by NWS Albuquerque NM",
                "description": "* WHAT...Visibility one quarter mile or less in freezing fog.\n\n* WHERE...Curry County, De Baca County, Guadalupe County, Roosevelt\nCounty, and Chaves County Plains.\n\n* WHEN...Until 8 AM MST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Low visibility could make driving conditions hazardous.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Areas of freezing fog have developed in Santa\nRosa, Roswell and Clovis based on the latest observations and\nwebcams. Travel along I-25 in the Santa Rosa area, U.S. 60 and 84\nfrom Yeso and Santa Rosa to the Texas border, U.S. 285 in Chaves\nCounty, and U.S. 70 from Roswell to Clovis may become hazardous.\nSlick spots may develop on elevated bridges and overpasses on these\nrespective highways.",
                "instruction": "If driving, slow down, use your headlights, and leave plenty of\ndistance ahead of you.",
                "response": "Avoid",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Storm Warning, north winds 25 to 35 kt with\ngusts up to 50 kt and seas 5 to 8 feet expected. For the Freezing Spray Advisory, from 10 PM this\nevening to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in freezing spray is hazardous.\nFreezing spray may render mechanical and electronic components\ninoperative. Ice accretion on decks and superstructures may\nresult in some loss of stability. Storm force winds and\nhazardous seas will capsize or damage vessels and reduce\nvisibility.",
                "instruction": "Mariners should prepare for ice accumulation on their vessel and\nconsider altering plans to avoid or mitigate these hazardous\nconditions.                 "senderName": "NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 1:48AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 22 below expected. For the Wind Advisory, northwest\nwinds 15 to 25 mph with gusts up to 55 mph expected.\n\n* WHERE...Avery, Buncombe, Henderson, Mitchell, and Yancey Counties,\nplus mountainous western portions of Burke, Caldwell, McDowell,\nPolk, and Rutherford counties.\n\n* WHEN...Until 1 PM EST this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result. The\ndangerously cold wind chills as low as 20 below zero could cause\nfrostbite on exposed skin in as little as 30 minutes. Frostbite\nand hypothermia will occur if unprotected skin is exposed to these\ntemperatures. Pets and livestock will be endangered by extreme\ncold if left unprotected for extended periods.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing, wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {

                        "NPWGSP"
                    ],

                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 1:48AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills as low as 5 below zero\nexpected.\n\n* WHERE...Northeast Georgia, the foothills and Piedmont of North\nCarolina, and the Upstate of South Carolina.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...The dangerously cold wind chills as low as 5 below zero\ncould result in hypothermia or frostbite if precautions are not\ntaken. Frostbite and hypothermia will occur if unprotected skin is\nexposed to these temperatures. Pets and livestock will be\nendangered by extreme cold if left unprotected for extended\nperiods.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing, wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors.                 "headline": "Heavy Freezing Spray Warning issued February 1 at 1:40AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Wakefield VA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Gale Warning, north winds 25 to 35 kt with gusts\nup to 45 kt and seas 6 to 11 ft. For the Heavy Freezing Spray\nWarning, heavy freezing spray at a rate of 2 cm per hour or\ngreater may rapidly accumulate on vessels.\n\n* WHERE...Coastal waters from Fenwick Island DE to Chincoteague\nVA out 20 nm and Coastal waters from Chincoteague to Parramore\nIsland VA out 20 nm.\n\n* WHEN...For the Gale Warning, until 10 PM EST this evening. For\nthe Heavy Freezing Spray Warning, until 1 PM EST this\nafternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in heavy freezing spray is\nhazardous. Freezing spray may render mechanical and electronic\ncomponents inoperative. Rapid ice accretion on decks and\nsuperstructures may result in a catastrophic loss of\nstability. Strong winds will cause hazardous seas which could\ncapsize or damage vessels and reduce visibility.",
                "instruction": "Mariners should prepare for dangerous accumulation of ice on\ntheir vessel. If possible, remain in port, avoid the warning area\nor conduct mitigation.\n\nMariners should alter plans to avoid these hazardous conditions.\nRemain in port, seek safe harbor, alter course, and/or secure the\nvessel for hazardous conditions.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Storm Warning, north winds 30 to 40 kt with\ngusts up to 50 kt and waves 5 to 8 ft.                 "headline": "Gale Warning issued February 1 at 1:40AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Wakefield VA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Gale Warning, north winds 20 to 30 kt with\ngusts up to 40 kt and waves 2 to 4 ft. For the Freezing Spray\nAdvisory, moderate accumulation of freezing spray.\n\n* WHERE...Rappahannock River from Urbanna to Windmill Point,\nYork River and James River from Jamestown to the James River\nBridge.\n\n* WHEN...Until 1 PM EST this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in freezing spray is hazardous.\nFreezing spray may render mechanical and electronic components\ninoperative. For\nthe Heavy Freezing Spray Warning, until 1 PM EST this\nafternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in heavy freezing spray is\nhazardous.                 "headline": "Wind Advisory issued February 1 at 1:35AM EST until February 1 at 4:00PM EST by NWS Blacksburg VA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 20 below expected. For the Wind Advisory, northwest\nwinds 15 to 25 mph with gusts up to 50 mph expected.\n\n* WHERE...In North Carolina, Alleghany NC, Ashe, and Watauga\nCounties. In Virginia, Carroll, Floyd, Grayson, and Roanoke\nCounties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST this\nafternoon. For the Wind Advisory, until 4 PM EST this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\n\nThe dangerously cold wind chills as low as 20 below zero could cause\nfrostbite on exposed skin in as little as 30 minutes. Frostbite and\nhypothermia will occur if unprotected skin is exposed to these\ntemperatures.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nIf you do not have heat in your home or lack shelter, you can call\n211 for assistance locating appropriate shelter from the cold.",

                "senderName": "NWS Upton NY",
                "headline": "Heavy Freezing Spray Advisory issued February 1 at 1:30AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Upton NY",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Freezing Spray Advisory, moderate accumulation\nof freezing spray. For the Gale Warning, north winds 20 to 30\nkt with gusts up to 40 kt and seas 3 to 5 feet.\n\n* WHERE...Long Island Sound.\n\n* WHEN...For the Freezing Spray Advisory, until 1 PM EST this\nafternoon. For the Gale Warning, until 6 PM EST this evening.\n\n* IMPACTS...Operating a vessel in freezing spray is hazardous.\nFreezing spray may render mechanical and electronic components\ninoperative. Ice accretion on decks and superstructures may\nresult in some loss of stability. Strong winds will cause\nhazardous seas which could capsize or damage vessels and\nreduce visibility.",
                "instruction": "Mariners should prepare for ice accumulation on their vessel and\nconsider altering plans to avoid or mitigate these hazardous\nconditions.\n\nMariners should alter plans to avoid these hazardous conditions.\nRemain in port, seek safe harbor, alter course, and/or secure the\nvessel for hazardous conditions.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 15.                 "event": "Coastal Flood Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Upton NY",
                "headline": "Coastal Flood Advisory issued February 1 at 1:14AM EST until February 1 at 12:00PM EST by NWS Upton NY",
                "description": "* WHAT...Up to one foot of inundation above ground level\nexpected in vulnerable areas near the waterfront and shoreline.\n\n* WHERE...Southwest Suffolk and Southeast Suffolk Counties.\n\n* WHEN...From 5 AM early this morning to noon EST today.\n\n* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in the more vulnerable locations near\nthe waterfront and shoreline. Some roads and low lying\nproperties including parking lots, parks, lawns, and homes and\nbusinesses with basements near the waterfront will experience\nminor flooding.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Additional minor coastal flooding is\npossible with high tide cycles late today through early Monday.\nIce from the back bays could get pushed onshore during high\ntide, and flood waters could freeze on roadways, further\ncomplicating impacts.",
                "instruction": "If travel is required, allow extra time as some roads may be\nclosed. Do not drive around barricades or through water of\nunknown depth. Take the necessary actions to protect flood-prone\nproperty.",

                "headline": "Freeze Warning issued January 31 at 11:47PM CST until February 1 at 10:00AM CST by NWS Lake Charles LA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Cold Weather Advisory, very cold wind chills as\nlow as 15. For the Freeze Warning, hard freeze with air\ntemperatures as low as 19 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of central, southwest, and west central\nLouisiana and southeast Texas.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM CST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frostbite and hypothermia will occur if unprotected\nskin is exposed to these temperatures. Frost and freeze\nconditions could kill crops, other sensitive vegetation and\npossibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Use caution while traveling outside.                 "event": "Cold Weather Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Lake Charles LA",
                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued January 31 at 11:47PM CST until February 1 at 10:00AM CST by NWS Lake Charles LA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Cold Weather Advisory, very cold wind chills as\nlow as 15. For the Freeze Warning, hard freeze with air\ntemperatures as low as 19 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of central, southwest, and west central\nLouisiana and southeast Texas.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM CST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frostbite and hypothermia will occur if unprotected\nskin is exposed to these temperatures. Frost and freeze\nconditions could kill crops, other sensitive vegetation and\npossibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Use caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them\nto drip slowly.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {

                "headline": "Special Weather Statement issued February 1 at 12:46AM EST by NWS Jackson KY",
                "description": "Scattered snow showers will stream from north to south across the\narea during the pre-dawn hours this morning. Fluffy snow\naccumulations of a coating to one-half inch are possible with the\nmost persistent activity. Slippery spots are possible on area\nroadways, especially on bridges and overpasses.\n\nThe area of more persistent snow shower activity will gradually\ntransition from between the Interstate 75 and US Highway 23\ncorridors to along the US Highway 23 and US Highway 119 corridors\nduring the pre-dawn hours this morning.",

                "senderName": "NWS Birmingham AL",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued January 31 at 11:43PM CST until February 1 at 12:00PM CST by NWS Birmingham AL",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills ranging from near zero into\nthe single digits are expected.\n\n* WHERE...Barbour, Bullock, Calhoun, Chambers, Cherokee, Clay,\nCleburne, Coosa, Elmore, Lee, Macon, Montgomery, Pike, Randolph,\nRussell, Talladega, and Tallapoosa Counties.\n\n* WHEN...Until noon CST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frostbite and hypothermia will occur if unprotected skin\nis exposed to these temperatures. An extended period of\nsubfreezing temperatures could cause pipes to burst.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTo prevent freezing and possible bursting of pipes they should be\nwrapped, drained, or allowed to drip slowly.",

                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 12:43AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Wakefield VA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the High Wind Warning, north winds 25 to 35 mph with\ngusts up to 60 mph. For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold\nwind chills as low as 5 below expected.\n\n* WHERE...In North Carolina, Eastern Currituck and Western Currituck\nCounties. In Virginia, Northampton County, Norfolk, Portsmouth,\nand Virginia Beach.\n\n* WHEN...For the High Wind Warning, until 4 PM EST this afternoon.\nFor the Extreme Cold Warning, until 10 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Damaging winds will blow down trees and power lines.\nWidespread power outages are expected. Travel will be difficult,\nespecially for high profile vehicles. Gusty winds will blow around\nunsecured objects. Frostbite and hypothermia will occur if\nunprotected skin is exposed to these temperatures. An extended\nperiod of freezing temperatures could cause ruptured water pipes.",
                "instruction": "Remain in the lower levels of your home during the windstorm, and\navoid windows. Watch for falling debris and tree limbs. Use caution\nif you must drive.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.",

                "senderName": "NWS Wilmington NC",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 12:24AM EST until February 2 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Wilmington NC",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills as low as zero.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of southeast North Carolina and northeast South\nCarolina.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...The dangerously cold wind chills could result in\nhypothermia or frostbite if precautions are not taken.",
                "instruction": "To prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.",
                "response": "Prepare",

                "event": "Cold Weather Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Morristown TN",
                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued February 1 at 12:15AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Morristown TN",
                "description": "* WHAT...Very cold wind chills as low as 5 above zero to 5 below\nzero expected at times over the Valley and Plateau as well as\nSouthwest North Carolina, while values as low as zero to 10 below\nzero can be expected at times over Southwest Virginia.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of southwest North Carolina, east Tennessee, and\nsouthwest Virginia.\n\n* WHEN...Until 1 PM EST /noon CST/ this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...The cold wind chills could result in hypothermia if\nprecautions are not taken.",
                "instruction": "Use caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWMRX"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS74 KMRX 010515"
                    ],
                    "NWSheadline": [

                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Morristown TN",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 12:15AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Morristown TN",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills as low as 20 below zero\nexpected.\n\n* WHERE...Blount Smoky Mountains, Cocke Smoky Mountains, Johnson,\nSevier Smoky Mountains, Southeast Carter, Southeast Greene,\nSoutheast Monroe, and Unicoi Counties.\n\n* WHEN...Until 1 PM EST this afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...The cold wind chills as low as 20 below zero could\nresult in hypothermia or frostbite if precautions are not taken.\nFrostbite and hypothermia will occur if unprotected skin is\nexposed to these temperatures.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.",
                "response": "Prepare",

                "event": "High Surf Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Tiyan GU",
                "headline": "High Surf Advisory issued February 1 at 3:09PM ChST until February 3 at 6:00AM ChST by NWS Tiyan GU",
                "description": "* WHAT...Large breaking waves of 8 to 11 feet and dangerous rip\ncurrents.\n\n* WHERE...For the High Surf Advisory, north facing reefs of the\nMarianas. For the High Rip Current Risk, north and east facing\nreefs.\n\n* WHEN...Until 6 AM ChST Tuesday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Dangerous swimming and surfing conditions and\nlocalized beach erosion. Rip currents can sweep even the best\nswimmers away from shore into deeper water.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...A pulse of northerly swell and a shear line\nproducing significant northeast swell continue to build surf\nalong north and east facing reefs. Hazardous levels of 9 feet\nfor north facing reefs and dangerous rip currents along north\nand east facing reefs are expected through late Monday night.\nThen the shear line is anticipated to weaken and the northerly\nswell diminishes Tuesday.",
                "instruction": "Rip currents will be life threatening. Inexperienced swimmers\nshould remain out of the water due to dangerous surf conditions.\n\nRip currents will be life threatening. If caught in a rip\ncurrent, relax and float. Don't swim against the current. If\nable, swim in a direction following the shoreline. If unable to\nescape, face the shore and call or wave for help.",
                "response": "Avoid",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "CFWGUM"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WHMY40 PGUM 010509"
                    ],
                    "NWSheadline": [
