                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Wilmington OH",
                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued February 1 at 12:07AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Wilmington OH",
                "description": "* WHAT...Very cold wind chills as low as 15 below zero.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of east central and southeast Indiana, northeast\nand northern Kentucky, and central, south central, southwest, and\nwest central Ohio.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM EST this morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...The cold wind chills as low as 15 below zero could\nresult in hypothermia or frostbite if precautions are not taken.\nVery cold temperatures can lead to hypothermia with prolonged\nexposure.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.",
                "response": "Prepare",

                "event": "Freeze Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "headline": "Freeze Warning issued February 1 at 12:02AM EST until February 2 at 9:00AM EST by NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 6 above. For the first Freeze Warning for a hard freeze,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 19. For the Cold Weather\nAdvisory, very cold wind chills as low as 16 expected. For the\nsecond Freeze Warning for a hard freeze, sub-freezing temperatures\nas low as 22 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of Big Bend and Panhandle Florida and south\ncentral and southwest Georgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST /noon CST/\nSunday. For the first Freeze Warning, until 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/\nSunday. For the Cold Weather Advisory, from 8 PM EST /7 PM CST/\nSunday to 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/ Monday. For the second Freeze\nWarning, from midnight EST /11 PM CST/ Sunday Night to 9 AM EST /8\nAM CST/ Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other\nsensitive vegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor\nplumbing.",
                "instruction": "Wear protective clothing such as a hat, facemask and heavy gloves or\nmittens if you have plans to be outdoors.\n\nTo prevent freezing and possible bursting of outdoor water pipes\nthey should be wrapped, drained, or allowed to drip slowly. Those\nthat have in-ground sprinkler systems should drain them and cover\nabove-ground pipes to protect them from freezing.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Execute",

                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles.                 "event": "Extreme Cold Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 12:02AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 10 above. For the Freeze Warning for a hard freeze,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 21. For the Wind Advisory,\nnorthwest winds 10 to 20 mph with gusts up to 40 mph. For the Cold\nWeather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 19 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Coastal Dixie, Coastal Franklin, Coastal Jefferson,\nCoastal Taylor, and Coastal Wakulla Counties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST Sunday. For\nthe Freeze Warning and Wind Advisory, until 10 AM EST Sunday. For\nthe Cold Weather Advisory, from 8 PM Sunday to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nWear protective clothing such as a hat, facemask and heavy gloves or\nmittens if you have plans to be outdoors.\n\nTo prevent freezing and possible bursting of outdoor water pipes\nthey should be wrapped, drained, or allowed to drip slowly. Those\nthat have in-ground sprinkler systems should drain them and cover\nabove-ground pipes to protect them from freezing.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWTAE"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS72 KTAE 010502"
                    ],

                "event": "Extreme Cold Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued February 1 at 12:02AM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 6 above. For the first Freeze Warning for a hard freeze,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 19. For the Cold Weather\nAdvisory, very cold wind chills as low as 16 expected. For the\nsecond Freeze Warning for a hard freeze, sub-freezing temperatures\nas low as 22 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of Big Bend and Panhandle Florida and south\ncentral and southwest Georgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST /noon CST/\nSunday. For the first Freeze Warning, until 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/\nSunday. For the Cold Weather Advisory, from 8 PM EST /7 PM CST/\nSunday to 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/ Monday. For the second Freeze\nWarning, from midnight EST /11 PM CST/ Sunday Night to 9 AM EST /8\nAM CST/ Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other\nsensitive vegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor\nplumbing.",
                "instruction": "Wear protective clothing such as a hat, facemask and heavy gloves or\nmittens if you have plans to be outdoors.\n\nTo prevent freezing and possible bursting of outdoor water pipes\nthey should be wrapped, drained, or allowed to drip slowly. Those\nthat have in-ground sprinkler systems should drain them and cover\nabove-ground pipes to protect them from freezing.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Prepare",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 10 above. For the Freeze Warning for a hard freeze,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 21. For the Wind Advisory,\nnorthwest winds 10 to 20 mph with gusts up to 40 mph. For the Cold\nWeather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 19 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Coastal Dixie, Coastal Franklin, Coastal Jefferson,\nCoastal Taylor, and Coastal Wakulla Counties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST Sunday. For\nthe Freeze Warning and Wind Advisory, until 10 AM EST Sunday. For\nthe Cold Weather Advisory, from 8 PM Sunday to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside.                 "event": "Freeze Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "headline": "Freeze Warning issued February 1 at 12:02AM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 6 above. For the first Freeze Warning for a hard freeze,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 19. For the Cold Weather\nAdvisory, very cold wind chills as low as 16 expected. For the\nsecond Freeze Warning for a hard freeze, sub-freezing temperatures\nas low as 22 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of Big Bend and Panhandle Florida and south\ncentral and southwest Georgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST /noon CST/\nSunday. For the first Freeze Warning, until 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/\nSunday. For the Cold Weather Advisory, from 8 PM EST /7 PM CST/\nSunday to 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/ Monday. For the second Freeze\nWarning, from midnight EST /11 PM CST/ Sunday Night to 9 AM EST /8\nAM CST/ Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other\nsensitive vegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor\nplumbing.",
                "instruction": "Wear protective clothing such as a hat, facemask and heavy gloves or\nmittens if you have plans to be outdoors.\n\nTo prevent freezing and possible bursting of outdoor water pipes\nthey should be wrapped, drained, or allowed to drip slowly. Those\nthat have in-ground sprinkler systems should drain them and cover\nabove-ground pipes to protect them from freezing.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Execute",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 10 above. For the Freeze Warning for a hard freeze,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 21. For the Wind Advisory,\nnorthwest winds 10 to 20 mph with gusts up to 40 mph. For the Cold\nWeather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 19 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Coastal Dixie, Coastal Franklin, Coastal Jefferson,\nCoastal Taylor, and Coastal Wakulla Counties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST Sunday. For\nthe Freeze Warning and Wind Advisory, until 10 AM EST Sunday. For\nthe Cold Weather Advisory, from 8 PM Sunday to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nWear protective clothing such as a hat, facemask and heavy gloves or\nmittens if you have plans to be outdoors.\n\nTo prevent freezing and possible bursting of outdoor water pipes\nthey should be wrapped, drained, or allowed to drip slowly. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside.                 "urgency": "Expected",
                "event": "Cold Weather Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued February 1 at 12:02AM EST until February 2 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning, dangerously cold wind chills\nas low as 6 above. For the first Freeze Warning for a hard freeze,\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as 19. For the Cold Weather\nAdvisory, very cold wind chills as low as 16 expected. For the\nsecond Freeze Warning for a hard freeze, sub-freezing temperatures\nas low as 22 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of Big Bend and Panhandle Florida and south\ncentral and southwest Georgia.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning, until 1 PM EST /noon CST/\nSunday. For the first Freeze Warning, until 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/\nSunday. For the Cold Weather Advisory, from 8 PM EST /7 PM CST/\nSunday to 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/ Monday. For the second Freeze\nWarning, from midnight EST /11 PM CST/ Sunday Night to 9 AM EST /8\nAM CST/ Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other\nsensitive vegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor\nplumbing.",
                "instruction": "Wear protective clothing such as a hat, facemask and heavy gloves or\nmittens if you have plans to be outdoors.\n\nTo prevent freezing and possible bursting of outdoor water pipes\nthey should be wrapped, drained, or allowed to drip slowly. Those\nthat have in-ground sprinkler systems should drain them and cover\nabove-ground pipes to protect them from freezing.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Prepare",

                    ],

                    ],

                "event": "Extreme Cold Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Peachtree City GA",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued January 31 at 11:54PM EST until February 1 at 1:00PM EST by NWS Peachtree City GA",
                "description": "* WHAT...Dangerously cold wind chills ranging from as cold as 15\ndegrees below in the north Georgia mountains to the single digits\nabove zero in central Georgia.\n\n* WHERE...All of north and central Georgia.\n\n* WHEN...Until 1 PM EST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Frostbite and hypothermia will occur if unprotected skin\nis exposed to these temperatures. An extended period of freezing\ntemperatures could cause ruptured water pipes.",
                "instruction": "Dress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must go\noutside.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWFFC"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS72 KFFC 010454"
                    ],

                "senderName": "NWS Tampa Bay Ruskin FL",
                "headline": "Cold Weather Advisory issued January 31 at 11:16PM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Tampa Bay Ruskin FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Freeze Warning tonight, sub-freezing temperatures\nas low as 28. For the Cold Weather Advisory tonight, very cold\nwind chills as low as 22. For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds\n15 to 25 mph with gusts up to 35 mph. For the Freeze Warning\nSunday night, sub-freezing temperatures as low as 28 expected. For\nthe Cold Weather Advisory Sunday night, very cold wind chills as\nlow as 24 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Inland Lee, Coastal Hillsborough, and Inland Sarasota\nCounties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Freeze Warning and Cold Weather Advisory tonight,\nvalid until 10 AM EST Sunday. For the Wind Advisory, valid until 7\nAM EST Sunday. For the Freeze Warning Sunday night, valid from 7\nPM Sunday to 9 AM EST Monday. For the Cold Weather Advisory Sunday\nnight, valid from 7 PM Sunday to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {

                "senderName": "NWS Tampa Bay Ruskin FL",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued January 31 at 11:16PM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Tampa Bay Ruskin FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Extreme Cold Warning tonight, dangerously cold wind\nchills as low as 8. For the Freeze Warning tonight, sub-freezing\ntemperatures as low as 20. For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds\n15 to 25 mph with gusts up to 35 mph. For the Freeze Warning\nSunday night, sub-freezing temperatures as low as 20 expected. For\nthe Cold Weather Advisory, very cold wind chills as low as 18\nexpected.\n\n* WHERE...Portions of southwest and west central Florida.\n\n* WHEN...For the Extreme Cold Warning and Freeze Warning tonight,\nvalid until 10 AM EST Sunday. For the Wind Advisory, valid until 7\nAM EST Sunday. For the Freeze Warning Sunday night, valid from 7\nPM Sunday to 9 AM EST Monday. For the Cold Weather Advisory Sunday\nnight, valid from 7 PM Sunday to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",

                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Tampa Bay Ruskin FL",
                "headline": "Wind Advisory issued January 31 at 11:16PM EST until February 1 at 7:00AM EST by NWS Tampa Bay Ruskin FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Freeze Warning tonight, sub-freezing temperatures\nas low as 28. For the Cold Weather Advisory tonight, very cold\nwind chills as low as 22. For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds\n15 to 25 mph with gusts up to 35 mph. For the Freeze Warning\nSunday night, sub-freezing temperatures as low as 28 expected. For\nthe Cold Weather Advisory Sunday night, very cold wind chills as\nlow as 24 expected.\n\n* WHERE...Inland Lee, Coastal Hillsborough, and Inland Sarasota\nCounties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Freeze Warning and Cold Weather Advisory tonight,\nvalid until 10 AM EST Sunday. For the Wind Advisory, valid until 7\nAM EST Sunday. For the Freeze Warning Sunday night, valid from 7\nPM Sunday to 9 AM EST Monday. For the Cold Weather Advisory Sunday\nnight, valid from 7 PM Sunday to 10 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",

                "headline": "Flood Warning issued January 31 at 9:58PM CST until February 2 at 6:00AM CST by NWS Lake Charles LA",
                "description": "...The Flood Warning continues for the following rivers in\nLouisiana...Texas...\n\nSabine River Near Deweyville\n\nAdditional information is available at www.weather.gov.\n\n* WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and minor flooding is forecast.\n\n* WHERE...Sabine River near Deweyville.\n\n* WHEN...Until Monday morning.\n\n* IMPACTS...At 24.0 feet, Minor lowland flooding will occur.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...\n- At 8:45 PM CST Saturday the stage was 24.2 feet.\n- Recent Activity...The maximum river stage in the 24 hours\nending at 8:45 PM CST Saturday was 24.3 feet.\n- Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage\ntomorrow evening and continue falling to 17.4 feet Thursday\nevening.\n- Flood stage is 24.0 feet.\n- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood",

                "senderName": "NWS Seattle WA",
                "headline": "Flood Watch issued January 31 at 7:37PM PST until February 2 at 10:00PM PST by NWS Seattle WA",
                "description": "* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is possible.\n\n* WHERE...A portion of northwest Washington, including the following\ncounty, Mason.\n\n* WHEN...From Sunday afternoon through Monday evening.\n\n* IMPACTS...Excessive runoff may result in flooding of rivers,\ncreeks, streams, and other low-lying and flood-prone locations.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...\n- Steadier rainfall at times tonight through Monday over the\nOlympic Peninsula will lead to rises on the Skokomish River,\nand the river will approach flood stage on Sunday.\n- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood",
                "instruction": "You should monitor later forecasts and be alert for possible Flood\nWarnings. Those living in areas prone to flooding should be prepared\nto take action should flooding develop.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the High Rip Current Risk, dangerous rip currents\nexpected.                 "instruction": "Inexperienced swimmers should remain out of the water due to\ndangerous surf conditions.\n\nSwim near a lifeguard. If caught in a rip current, relax and\nfloat.                 "event": "Coastal Flood Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS San Francisco CA",
                "headline": "Coastal Flood Advisory issued January 31 at 6:50PM PST until February 1 at 12:00PM PST by NWS San Francisco CA",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Beach Hazards Statement, an increased risk of\nsneaker waves and strong rip currents with breaking waves of\n14 to 19 feet. For the Coastal Flood Advisory, up to 1.3 ft of\ninundation above ground level is possible in low-lying areas\nnear shorelines and tidal waterways.\n\n* WHERE...San Francisco, the Monterey Bay and Big Sur Coast.\n\n* WHEN...For the Coastal Flood Advisory, until noon PST Sunday.\nFor the Beach Hazards Statement, through 9 AM PST Monday\nmorning.\n\n* IMPACTS...Flooding of lots, parks, and roads with only\nisolated road closures expected. Dangerous conditions are\nforecast along the shoreline. Hazards include sneaker waves,\nstrong rip currents, and large breaking waves.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...At the San Francisco tidal gauge, high tide\nis expected to be: 1.2 ft above normal (6.96 ft MLLW) at 10:22\nAM Sunday 2/1. These predictions include up to 0.3 ft of storm\nsurge from an incoming moderate to long period swell that will\nenhance the astronomical tide and flooding threat. High tide\ntiming varies up to 90 minutes earlier or later along the\nPacific Coast and through the San Francisco Bay, respectively.",
                "instruction": "If travel is required, allow extra time as some roads may be\nclosed. Do not drive around barricades or through water of\nunknown depth. Take the necessary actions to protect flood-prone\nproperty.\n\nStay off of jetties, piers, rocks, and other waterside\ninfrastructure. Remain out of the water to avoid hazardous surf\nand NEVER turn your back on the ocean. Monitor local weather,\nsurf and tide forecasts at www.weather.gov/mtr.",
                "response": "Monitor",

                "messageType": "Update",

                "severity": "Minor",

                "urgency": "Expected",
                "event": "High Surf Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Honolulu HI",
                "headline": "High Surf Advisory issued January 31 at 4:07PM HST until February 1 at 6:00AM HST by NWS Honolulu HI",
                "description": "...HIGH SURF ADVISORY IN EFFECT UNTIL 6 AM HST SUNDAY MORNING FOR\nNORTH AND WEST FACING SHORES OF NIIHAU KAUAI OAHU AND\nMOLOKAI...AND FOR NORTH FACING SHORES OF MAUI AND WEST FACING\nSHORES OF THE BIG ISLAND...\n\n...COASTAL FLOOD STATEMENT IN EFFECT FOR ALL HAWAIIAN ISLANDS...\n\n.Large surf will persist overnight due to overlapping west-\nnorthwest to northwest (295 to 320 degrees) swells. These swells\nwill continue to decline tonight into Sunday. Additionally,\nhigher than predicted tides will produce minor flooding along\nvulnerable low- lying coastal infrastructure, as well as cause\nsome beach erosion, during peak high tides between midnight and\nsunrise. The elevated tides will also contribute to runup and\nbeach erosion late tonight into early Sunday morning along north\nand west shores exposed to the large swell.\n\n* WHAT...For north facing shores of Maui, surf of 12 to 16 feet,\nslowly decreasing late tonight. For west facing shores of the\nBig Island, surf of 6 to 8 feet, slowly decreasing late tonight.\nAdditionally, a combination of higher than predicted tides and\nelevated surf will produce nuisance coastal flooding, mainly\nduring peak high tides in the early morning hours between\nmidnight and sunrise.\n\n* WHERE...North facing shores of Maui and west facing shores of\nthe Big Island.\n\n* WHEN...For the High Surf Advisory, until 6 AM HST Sunday. For\nthe Coastal Flood Statement, through Monday afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Moderate. Strong breaking waves and strong currents\nwill make swimming dangerous. Flooding of beaches that are\nnormally dry, minor coastal erosion, and saltwater inundation.",
                "instruction": "Avoid driving through flooded roadways. If you are forced to\ndrive through salt water, be sure to rinse your vehicle with\nfresh water. Move electronics, vehicles or other valuables to\nhigher ground. Monitor vessels to ensure mooring lines don't get\ntoo tight and watch out for overwash around boat ramps. Secure\ncanoes or other watercraft stowed on beaches.\n\nHeed all advice from ocean safety officials. When in doubt, don't\ngo out.",
                "response": "Avoid",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "CFWHFO"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WHHW40 PHFO 010207"
                    ],

                "event": "Coastal Flood Statement",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Honolulu HI",
                "headline": "Coastal Flood Statement issued January 31 at 4:07PM HST until February 2 at 6:00PM HST by NWS Honolulu HI",
                "description": "...HIGH SURF ADVISORY IN EFFECT UNTIL 6 AM HST SUNDAY MORNING FOR\nNORTH AND WEST FACING SHORES OF NIIHAU KAUAI OAHU AND\nMOLOKAI...AND FOR NORTH FACING SHORES OF MAUI AND WEST FACING\nSHORES OF THE BIG ISLAND...\n\n...COASTAL FLOOD STATEMENT IN EFFECT FOR ALL HAWAIIAN ISLANDS...\n\n.Large surf will persist overnight due to overlapping west-\nnorthwest to northwest (295 to 320 degrees) swells. These swells\nwill continue to decline tonight into Sunday. Additionally,\nhigher than predicted tides will produce minor flooding along\nvulnerable low- lying coastal infrastructure, as well as cause\nsome beach erosion, during peak high tides between midnight and\nsunrise. The elevated tides will also contribute to runup and\nbeach erosion late tonight into early Sunday morning along north\nand west shores exposed to the large swell.\n\n* WHAT...For north facing shores, surf of 18 to 24 feet, slowly\ndecreasing late tonight. For west facing shores, surf of 12 to\n16 feet, slowly decreasing late tonight. Additionally, a\ncombination of higher than predicted tides and elevated surf\nwill produce nuisance coastal flooding, mainly during peak high\ntides in the early morning hours between midnight and sunrise.\n\n* WHERE...North and west facing shores of Niihau, Kauai, Oahu,\nand Molokai.\n\n* WHEN...For the High Surf Advisory, until 6 AM HST Sunday. For\nthe Coastal Flood Statement, through Monday afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Moderate. Strong breaking waves and strong currents\nwill make swimming dangerous. Flooding of beaches that are\nnormally dry, minor coastal erosion, and saltwater inundation.",
                "instruction": "Avoid driving through flooded roadways. If you are forced to\ndrive through salt water, be sure to rinse your vehicle with\nfresh water. Move electronics, vehicles or other valuables to\nhigher ground. Monitor vessels to ensure mooring lines don't get\ntoo tight and watch out for overwash around boat ramps. Secure\ncanoes or other watercraft stowed on beaches.\n\nHeed all advice from ocean safety officials. When in doubt, don't\ngo out.",
                "response": "Monitor",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "CFWHFO"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WHHW40 PHFO 010207"
                    ],

                "headline": "Beach Hazards Statement issued January 31 at 2:18PM PST until February 2 at 4:00PM PST by NWS Portland OR",
                "description": "* WHAT...Sneaker waves possible.\n\n* WHERE...North and Central Coast of Oregon, and South Washington\nCoast.\n\n* WHEN...Through Monday afternoon.\n\n* IMPACTS...Waves can run up significantly farther on a beach than\nnormal, including over rocks and jetties. Sneaker waves can\nsuddenly knock people off of their feet and quickly pull them\ninto the frigid ocean which may lead to serious injury or\ndrowning.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Keep children and pets away from the surf\nzone. Keep off of jetties, rocks and logs near the surf zone. If\nyou see someone swept into the sea do not swim in after them.\nCall 911 and keep an eye on them until help arrives.",

                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "headline": "Gale Warning issued January 31 at 2:49PM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Tallahassee FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...Northwest winds 25 to 35 kt with gusts up to 45 kt and\nseas 7 to 12 ft.\n\n* WHERE...Offshore Gulf waters from the Walton-Okaloosa County\nline to the Ochlockonee River from 20 to 60 nautical miles\nout, Nearshore Gulf waters from the Ochlockonee River to the\nSuwannee River out 20 nautical miles, St. Andrews Bay\nWaterways, Coastal waters from Mexico Beach to Okaloosa Walton\nCounty Line FL out 20 NM, Coastal waters from Mexico Beach to\nApalachicola FL out 20 NM, and Coastal Waters From Ochlockonee\nRiver to Apalachicola Fl out to 20 Nm.\n\n* WHEN...Until 10 AM EST /9 AM CST/ Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Strong winds will cause hazardous seas which could\ncapsize or damage vessels and reduce visibility.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...There will be occasional light freezing\nspray after midnight tonight into Sunday morning for the shallow\nshelf waters close to the coast, including Apalachee Bay,\nOchlockonee Bay, Saint George Sound, Apalachicola Bay, St.\nJoseph Bay, and St. Andrews Bay. This may result in a light\ncoating of ice on vessels, especially smaller ones, as well as\non docks which may lead to slippery surfaces. For St. Andrews\nBay, winds will be 20 to 30 kt with gusts 35 to 40 kt. Protected\nwaters will be very rough.",
                "instruction": "Mariners should alter plans to avoid these hazardous conditions.\nRemain in port, seek safe harbor, alter course, and/or secure the\nvessel for severe conditions.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Coastal Flood Warning, 2 to 4 feet of\ninundation above ground level expected in low-lying areas near\nshorelines and tidal waterways. For the High Surf Advisory,\nlarge breaking waves of 8 to 12 feet expected in the surf zone.\n\n* WHERE...Northern Outer Banks and Hatteras Island.\n\n* WHEN...For the Coastal Flood Warning, until midnight EST\nSunday night. For the High Surf Advisory, from 7 PM this\nevening to 7 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Up to 2 to 4 feet of inundation above ground level\nis possible in some areas near shorelines and tidal waterways\nresulting in an elevated threat of property damage. Ocean overwash may lead to\ninundation of vulnerable portions of the coast, especially\naround times of high tides.                 "senderName": "NWS Newport/Morehead City NC",
                "headline": "Coastal Flood Warning issued January 31 at 2:27PM EST until February 2 at 12:00AM EST by NWS Newport/Morehead City NC",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Coastal Flood Warning, 2 to 4 feet of\ninundation above ground level expected in low-lying areas near\nshorelines and tidal waterways. For the High Surf Advisory,\nlarge breaking waves of 8 to 12 feet expected in the surf zone.\n\n* WHERE...Northern Outer Banks and Hatteras Island.\n\n* WHEN...For the Coastal Flood Warning, until midnight EST\nSunday night. For the High Surf Advisory, from 7 PM this\nevening to 7 AM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Up to 2 to 4 feet of inundation above ground level\nis possible in some areas near shorelines and tidal waterways\nresulting in an elevated threat of property damage. Flooding\nwill extend inland from the waterfront threatening some homes\nand businesses. Numerous road closures and flooding of\nvehicles will be possible. Ocean overwash may lead to\ninundation of vulnerable portions of the coast, especially\naround times of high tides. Portions of Highway 12 could\nbecome inundated and impassable at times of high tides.",
                "instruction": "Take the necessary actions to protect flood-prone property. If\ntravel is required, do not drive around barricades or through\nwater of unknown depth.\n\nInexperienced swimmers should remain out of the water due to\ndangerous surf conditions.",
                "response": "Avoid",

                "event": "Freeze Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Miami FL",
                "headline": "Freeze Warning issued January 31 at 1:59PM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Miami FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds 15 to 25 mph with\ngusts up to 45 mph expected. For the Extreme Cold Warning,\ndangerously cold wind chills as low as the middle to upper teens\nexpected. For the Freeze Warning, a HARD FREEZE due to\nsub-freezing temperatures as low as\nthe mid to upper 20s expected.\n\n* WHERE...Glades and Hendry Counties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Wind Advisory, until 7 AM EST Sunday. For the\nExtreme Cold Warning and Freeze Warning, from 10 PM this evening\nto 10 AM EST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMonitor the latest forecasts and warnings for updates.\n\nIf you do not have heat in your home or lack shelter, you can call\n211.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.",
                "response": "Execute",
                "parameters": {
                    "AWIPSidentifier": [
                        "NPWMFL"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [
                        "WWUS72 KMFL 311859"
                    ],

                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles.                 "event": "Extreme Cold Warning",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Miami FL",
                "headline": "Extreme Cold Warning issued January 31 at 1:59PM EST until February 1 at 10:00AM EST by NWS Miami FL",
                "description": "* WHAT...For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds 15 to 25 mph with\ngusts up to 45 mph expected. For the Extreme Cold Warning,\ndangerously cold wind chills as low as the upper teens to lower\n20s expected. For the Freeze Warning, sub-freezing temperatures as\nlow as the upper 20s to lower 30s expected.\n\n* WHERE...Coastal Palm Beach County, Inland Palm Beach County, and\nMetro Palm Beach County Counties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Wind Advisory, until 7 AM EST Sunday. For the\nExtreme Cold Warning and Freeze Warning, from 10 PM this evening\nto 10 AM EST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nDress in layers including a hat, face mask, and gloves if you must\ngo outside.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMonitor the latest forecasts and warnings for updates.\n\nIf you do not have heat in your home or lack shelter, you can call\n211.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.",
                "response": "Prepare",
                "parameters": {

                        "NPWMFL"
                    ],
                    "WMOidentifier": [

                    ],

                "description": "* WHAT...For the Wind Advisory, northwest winds 15 to 25 mph with\ngusts up to 45 mph expected. Sub-freezing temperatures as low as\nthe upper 20s to lower 30s expected. Apparent temperatures as low\nas the low to mid 20s.\n\n* WHERE...Inland Broward County, Inland Miami-Dade County, and\nInland Collier County Counties.\n\n* WHEN...For the Wind Advisory, until 7 AM EST Sunday. For the\nFreeze Warning and Cold Weather Advisory, from 10 PM this evening\nto 10 AM EST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree\nlimbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result. Wind\nchill values can lead to hypothermia with prolonged exposure.\nFrost and freeze conditions could kill crops, other sensitive\nvegetation and possibly damage unprotected outdoor plumbing.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.\n\nTo prevent water pipes from freezing; wrap or drain or allow them to\ndrip slowly.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.",

                "description": "* WHAT...For the High Rip Current Risk, dangerous rip currents\nexpected. For the High Surf Advisory,\nfrom 1 PM this afternoon to 7 PM EST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Dangerous swimming and surfing conditions and\nlocalized beach erosion.                 "instruction": "Inexperienced swimmers should remain out of the water due to\ndangerous surf conditions.\n\nSwim near a lifeguard. If caught in a rip current, relax and\nfloat.                 "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Grand Rapids MI",
                "headline": "Flood Advisory issued January 30 at 4:15PM EST until February 2 at 7:00PM EST by NWS Grand Rapids MI",
                "description": "* WHAT...Flooding along the Muskegon River remains possible due to\nan ice jam.\n\n* WHERE...A portion of west central Michigan, including the\nfollowing county, Newaygo.\n\n* WHEN...Until 700 PM EST Monday.\n\n* IMPACTS...Ice jams are notoriously unpredictable. Low-lying areas\nalong the Muskegon River that are prone to flooding may see\nrapidly changing water levels as ice shifts and adjusts.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...\n- At 408 PM EST, an ice jam remains in place on the Muskegon\nriver in southern Newaygo County. The river gauge at Newaygo\nshows water levels remaining at minor flood stage as of\nFriday afternoon. As temperatures warm this weekend, water\nlevels should continue to slowly fall. Shorter periods of\nrising and falling water levels are expected with any shifts\nin the ice.\n- As the river freezes up, ice can restrict the flow of the\nriver and cause rises and falls in water level. While\nflooding associated with freeze up ice jams is typically\nminor, conditions can change rapidly and unpredictably.\n- Some locations that will experience flooding include...\nNewaygo.\n- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood",
                "instruction": "Please report observed flooding to local emergency services or law\nenforcement. Respect any road closures in the area.",
                "response": "Avoid",

                "headline": "The Extreme Cold Watch has been replaced. Please see the latest information from NWS Tallahassee FL on this developing situation.",
                "description": "The Extreme Cold Watch has been replaced. Please see the latest information from NWS Tallahassee FL on this developing situation.",
                "instruction": "Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high\nprofile vehicles. Use extra caution.\n\nSecure outdoor objects.\n\nWear protective clothing such as a hat, facemask and heavy gloves or\nmittens if you have plans to be outdoors.\n\nTo prevent freezing and possible bursting of outdoor water pipes\nthey should be wrapped, drained, or allowed to drip slowly. Those\nthat have in-ground sprinkler systems should drain them and cover\nabove-ground pipes to protect them from freezing.\n\nKeep pets indoors as much as possible.\n\nMake sure outdoor animals have a warm, dry shelter, food, and\nunfrozen water.\n\nMake frequent checks on older family, friends, and neighbors. Ensure\nportable heaters are used correctly. Do not use generators or grills\ninside.\n\nUse caution while traveling outside. Wear appropriate clothing, a\nhat, and gloves.\n\nTake steps now to protect tender plants from the cold.",

                "event": "Flood Advisory",
                "sender": "w-nws.webmaster@noaa.gov",
                "senderName": "NWS Grand Junction CO",
                "headline": "Flood Advisory issued January 29 at 12:10PM MST until February 1 at 8:00PM MST by NWS Grand Junction CO",
                "description": "* WHAT...Rapid rise in water levels and potential river flooding\ncaused by an ice jam release is possible.\n\n* WHERE...The Roaring Fork River and Fryingpan River from Snowmass\nCanyon through the town of Basalt and downstream to the confluence\nwith the Colorado River.\n\n* WHEN...Until 800 PM MST Sunday.\n\n* IMPACTS...A river ice jam release could result in a rapid increase\nin water levels and flow. Anyone near or in the Roaring Fork River\nor Fryingpan River should use extreme caution through the weekend.\nRivers can fill with ice and debris in a matter of minutes\ndownstream a river ice break.\n\n* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...\n- At 1107 AM MST, Emergency Management reported a river ice jam\nwithin the advisory area. Warmer weather forecast through the\nweekend could allow river ice jams to break and result in\nminor flooding downstream.\n- Some locations that may experience flooding include...\nCarbondale, Basalt, Snowmass, Snowmass Village, Emma, and El\nJebel.\n- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood",
                "instruction": "Please report observed flooding to local emergency services or law\nenforcement and request they pass this information to the National\nWeather Service when you can do so safely.\n\nEmergency management and law enforcement are asking all citizens to\nuse extreme caution in and around the Roaring Fork River and\nFryingpan River.",
